seo.title: 100 AI Blog Post Ideas for Next.js Developers
seo.description: Discover 100 AI-powered blog post ideas tailored for Next.js developers. From App Router patterns to AI SDK integrations, fuel your content calendar.

content.intro: Next.js developers have a unique intersection of topics to write about: the rapidly evolving App Router, server components, edge deployment, and now AI integration. Whether you're building your personal dev blog or a technical content site, these 100 ideas span the full spectrum from beginner tutorials to advanced architectural deep-dives.

items.title: How to migrate from Pages Router to App Router without breaking production
items.description: A step-by-step migration guide with real-world pitfalls and rollback strategies.

items.title: Server Components vs. Client Components: the definitive decision framework
items.description: When to use each, with a flowchart developers can bookmark and reference.

items.title: Nested layouts in Next.js App Router explained with real examples

items.title: Data fetching patterns in Next.js 15: fetch, cache, and revalidate demystified

items.title: Building a streaming UI with React Suspense in Next.js App Router
items.description: How to progressively load server-rendered content without client-side spinners.

items.title: Error boundaries and error.tsx in Next.js App Router

items.title: Loading.tsx and Suspense: how skeleton screens work in App Router
items.description: The mechanics behind automatic loading states and how to customize them.

items.title: Next.js route handlers vs. API routes: when to use which
items.description: Comparing the two approaches with performance, complexity, and use-case analysis.

items.title: Parallel routes for dashboard layouts in Next.js
items.description: Building complex dashboard UIs with independently loading panels.

items.title: Intercepting routes for modal patterns in Next.js App Router
items.description: How to open a modal while keeping the underlying page URL shareable.

items.title: Adding OpenAI streaming to a Next.js app in 15 minutes

items.title: Vercel AI SDK vs. raw fetch: which to use for Next.js AI apps

items.title: Building a RAG app with Next.js and Supabase pgvector
items.description: End-to-end guide: document ingestion, embeddings, retrieval, and generation.

items.title: How to secure your OpenAI API key in a Next.js app
items.description: Route handlers, environment variables, and rate limiting to protect your API budget.

items.title: Streaming AI chat with Next.js Server Actions
items.description: Using server actions for AI responses instead of API routes — pros and cons.

items.title: Building a structured output pipeline with Next.js and Gemini
items.description: Using Gemini's native JSON mode with Zod validation in a production Next.js app.

items.description: Replacing keyword search with semantic search using pgvector and cosine similarity.

pro_tips: Write the tutorial you wish had existed when you were learning — your past self is your best audience proxy.
Use real error messages and production gotchas in your titles; developers search for exact error strings.
A comparative article (A vs. B) consistently outperforms tutorials because it captures decision-phase searchers.
Update articles when Next.js releases a major version — 'updated for Next.js 15' gets click-throughs from loyal readers.
Include a GitHub repo with working code for every implementation guide; it becomes a long-term referral link.
